title: Being an Evangelion fan
wikitext:
[]: Being an Evangelion fan.             Quote Post by Shinato » Sun Jun 24, 2012 5:47 pm I was asked the other day by a friend what I regarded as the most influential anime of time, I listed a few contenders but ultimately said NGE. He stopped me promptly thereafter to tell me that all NGE has ever influenced is its "rabid fan base". Needless to say an argument was had where I demanded he explain how that's the only thing the series has ever accomplished, after yelling for a few minutes he finally admitted that he had never actually watched the series in its entirety. I was really angry that he had carried on like he did for that to be his reply but the argument quickly subsided and we left well enough alone and ended the discussion. Afterwards I did some reading and apparently he is not the only one with this sentiment. Ever since I have been noticing that there is almost a stigma that surrounds NGE and its fans among "True Anime" fans. I just don't understand why my high regard and resolve for this series to be recognized gets me shunned or looked upon as someone who doesn't "really enjoy" anime. It's not like I, or other NGE fans shove the series down throats. I mean, here is a board that has been up for a number of years for die hard and newly introduced NGE fans to connect. We're not (pardon the pun) evangelists looking to convert others to this series. I guess I just wanted to see what it means to everyone to be a NGE fan and how, if you are, it affects how you're perceived outside of the NGE fan base.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Sun Jun 24, 2012 6:45 pm The stigma is somewhat well-deserved. There will always be embarassing portions of the fanbase - not just of Eva, but of any popular media - that act like what they're consuming is the second coming of Christ, the best story ever told. These people also tend to be loud and embarassingly memorable. And certainly Eva does get shoved down people's throats, though less so nowadays. Try telling someone partial to anime that you haven't seen it and they'll scoff. It's not unusual that people would build an opinion based on the annoyance they feel for an overbearing fanbase. Without a doubt, Evangelion has been influential on its industry. It's an interesting piece with genuine artistic merit. It says something that it still provokes discussion among viewers today. It's not perfect. But no human endeavor really is.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinato » Sun Jun 24, 2012 7:02 pm That's well put. I've really thought about all of that before but I still have trouble seeing it. You said yourself there's an embarrassing part of any fan base that considers what they like to be holier than thou. I just have a hard time believing die hard NGE fans are worse than die hard fans of anything else. I really can't think of another anime that is shunned or disregarded because of its fanbase (Unless you want to get into Naruto or something like that). I would talk down any NGE fan if I saw/heard them bullying someone based off them not seeing the series and from what I can see so would the majority of people on this site/forum.

[]:             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Sun Jun 24, 2012 8:36 pm The smallest portion of a fandom is also the loudest.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Jun 24, 2012 9:50 pm Shinato wrote: You said yourself there's an embarrassing part of any fan base that considers what they like to be holier than thou. I just have a hard time believing die hard NGE fans are worse than die hard fans of anything else.

[]:             Quote Post by Brainman » Sun Jun 24, 2012 11:18 pm In other words, stop worrying about it.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinato » Mon Jun 25, 2012 12:51 am esselfortium wrote: Give it time. You've only been here a day, after all.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jun 25, 2012 10:33 am I usually find that if I can't crack a joke about a show or its director, then I'm probably taking it way too seriously. I find that the same is true with others. If I crack a joke about how Joss Whedon's camera work in introduction of Dollhouse looked like it was a big budget "As The World Turns," the rabid annoying Mutant Enemy fans become apparent really fast. That being said, the only two shows I can't really crack jokes about are Cowboy Bebop , mainly because the director already took the fun out of making fun of it by poking at it himself, and Mister Rogers' Neighborhood , because MISTER ROGERS SAID HE LIKES ME JUST THE WAY I AM! HE SAID THAT I WAS ONE IN A MILLION! AND HE FED FISH AND IT WAS AWESOME!!!!! Seriously though, any Mister Rogers' joke is uncalled for. I really respect and enjoy both the show and the creators of Neon Genesis Evangelion . In fact, their one of my favorites of all time. But that doesn't stop me from poking fun at it occasionally. After all, I'm not a rabid fan of it or anything like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinato » Mon Jun 25, 2012 1:12 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I usually find that if I can't crack a joke about a show or its director, then I'm probably taking it way too seriously. I find that the same is true with others. If I crack a joke about how Joss Whedon's camera work in introduction of Dollhouse looked like it was a big budget "As The World Turns," the rabid annoying Mutant Enemy fans become apparent really fast. That being said, the only two shows I can't really crack jokes about are Cowboy Bebop , mainly because the director already took the fun out of making fun of it by poking at it himself, and Mister Rogers' Neighborhood , because MISTER ROGERS SAID HE LIKES ME JUST THE WAY I AM! HE SAID THAT I WAS ONE IN A MILLION! AND HE FED FISH AND IT WAS AWESOME!!!!! Seriously though, any Mister Rogers' joke is uncalled for. I really respect and enjoy both the show and the creators of Neon Genesis Evangelion . In fact, their one of my favorites of all time. But that doesn't stop me from poking fun at it occasionally. After all, I'm not a rabid fan of it or anything like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Jun 25, 2012 4:20 pm all die hard fans are annoying. What's grating about eva fans in a way that isn't annoying about, say, toku fans is the general pretentiousness and try hard spirit just look at that Destrado guy on /a/, I wouldn't be surprised if one of guys here was behind his trip.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Mon Jun 25, 2012 5:09 pm /a/ actually has fairly reasonable threads these days when it isn't busy turning them into an elaborate courtship ritual of pre-defined trollings. I'm noticing the same "YEAH BUT IT'S A SMART CARTOON" attitude you're talking about from Madoka diehards, lately.

[]:             Quote Post by Butterscotch » Mon Jun 25, 2012 5:57 pm In the end of the day it is the person who is judgemental towards your interests is the one wth the problem not you.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Mon Jun 25, 2012 6:03 pm Not really? Sometimes it pays to look at why people are criticizing you with an objective eye.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Jun 25, 2012 6:12 pm Der Kommissar wrote: /a/ actually has fairly reasonable threads these days when it isn't busy turning them into an elaborate courtship ritual of pre-defined trollings. I'm noticing the same "YEAH BUT IT'S A SMART CARTOON" attitude you're talking about from Madoka diehards, lately.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinato » Mon Jun 25, 2012 10:06 pm So, I get the feeling that there's a general consensus that NGE is an anime with a more "rabid" fan base? I don't see how this is really fair. With it being a thought provoking series I can really see how a few less than stellar fellows might use it as a way to massage their egos but it still just doesn't really seem all that deserved. I guess the question still stands, what does it mean to be a NGE fan and how, if it does, it affects how you're perceived outside of the NGE fan base.

[]: It's a beautiful day to DIE! >BO             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Jun 26, 2012 12:41 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Seriously though, any Mister Rogers' joke is uncalled for.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jun 26, 2012 1:12 am He was never a sniper, it was one of those things that someone made up and ended up on those "Facts about Mr. Rogers" internet lists, which many of those facts were unconfirmed by personal accounts like interviews by Tom Junod. There's a couple of related threads that have been around, one in particular which I can't seem to find discusses some of the perception of the fandom. http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/5231/Is-there-an-Evangelion-Backlash-in-fandom/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/8977/We-should-take-Eva-less-seriously/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/4909/Has-Evangelion-fandom-gone-too-far/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/12043/Evangelion-and-the-general-Anime-community/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/1448/How-could-you-not-like-EVA/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/11857/EVA-The-Most-Divisive-Anime-Ever/

[]:             Quote Post by Shinato » Tue Jun 26, 2012 1:27 am Oh, I didn't mean to beat a dead horse or anything. Sorry if this is just adding to the list. ^^; Alaska, I really like how you put that, I know it's what I did. I guess that is why I seem to be at a loss on the subject.

[]: Mister Rogers is a *sigh*, Saint. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Jun 26, 2012 4:09 am Ornette wrote: He was never a sniper,

[]: Re: It's a beautiful day to DIE! >BO             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jun 26, 2012 10:27 am Alaska Slim wrote: Of my own opinion, a "true" fan is someone who watched Eva, took something meaningful from it, then simply walked away.
